prerequisites: A Microsoft Entra ID tenant. Note its Directory (tenant) ID for the connector's tenant_id field.
Permission to register an app in the Microsoft Entra admin center. Cloud Application Administrator is the least privileged role supported.
An administrator who can grant admin consent, since Microsoft Graph application permissions always require it.
The user_id (Microsoft Entra user identifier) the connector should read, available from the Microsoft Graph /users endpoint.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (app-only)
authMethods.summary: App-only access to Microsoft Graph using the OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant. The connector authenticates with its registered application's client_id, client_secret, and tenant_id, requesting the https://graph.microsoft.com/.default scope to get a bearer token. No signed-in user is involved; application permissions must be admin-consented.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center (https://entra.microsoft.com), go to Identity > Applications > App registrations, and select New registration. Enter a display name and choose the supported account types, then select Register.

setupSteps.title: Record the client_id and tenant_id
setupSteps.body: On the app registration's Overview pane, copy the Application (client) ID into the connector's client_id field and the Directory (tenant) ID into the tenant_id field.

setupSteps.title: Create a client secret
setupSteps.body: Go to Certificates & secrets > Client secrets > New client secret, add a description and expiration (limited to 24 months or less), and select Add. Copy the secret value immediately, since it is never displayed again, into the connector's client_secret field.

setupSteps.title: Add and consent to Microsoft Graph application permissions
setupSteps.body: On the API permissions page, select Add a permission > Microsoft Graph > Application permissions, then add the permissions the connector needs (for example, reading user data requires an application permission such as User.Read.All). An administrator must grant admin consent, because application permissions always require it.

setupSteps.title: Provide the target user_id
setupSteps.body: Identify the Microsoft Entra user to read by querying the Microsoft Graph /users endpoint, then set that user's id as the connector's user_id.
